programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: Diamond Collar

programme.title: Diamond Collar

programme.title: Louisiana Law
programme.desc: Wildlife agents interrogate a felon caught hunting with a firearm; witnesses report a hunter trying to bludgeon a swimming deer from his boat; a duck shot out of season leads agents to a slew of violations.

programme.title: Louisiana Law
programme.desc: Wildlife agents join a manhunt for an armed fugitive on the bayou; a nervous young hunter reveals a serious violation; a night shrimping bust leads to a familiar and unrepentant culprit.

programme.title: Louisiana Law

programme.title: Louisiana Law
programme.desc: Wildlife agents question a hunter with something to hide and notice damning evidence on his person; a group of youths shooting ducks from a moving boat is caught in the act; frightened hunters accuse a landowner of serial harassment.

programme.title: Louisiana Law

programme.desc: The development and sophistication of AI-manipulated video; both the New York Times and President Donald Trump are at odds over the truth; an investigation into the fight against fake news.

programme.title: Elvis Presley: The Searcher
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary about the singer, beginning with his return home after his discharge from the army, and how he dealt with a rapidly changing pop scene.

programme.title: I Am Patrick Swayze
programme.desc: A tribute to the American actor, known for his roles in hits like Dirty Dancing, Ghost and The Outsiders. With contributions by Demi Moore and Rob Lowe among others.

programme.title: Becoming Madonna
programme.desc: Documentary charting the singer's rise to stardom, built around a cache of recently uncovered audio tapes in which a young Madonna reflects on her extraordinary life and career. Plus, rare footage, unseen photos and interviews with family, friends and collaborators.

programme.title: Salt Path: A Very British Scandal
programme.desc: A look at Observer journalist Chloe Hadjimatheou's investigation into allegations of factual inaccuracies in Raynor Winn's book The Salt Path.

programme.title: The Wimbledon Kidnapping
programme.desc: A look back at the mysterious disappearance of Muriel McKay in Wimbledon in 1969, as well as the trial of the first kidnap and ransom case in Britain.

programme.title: Kurt Cobain: Montage of Heck
programme.desc: Filmmaker Brett Morgen explores the Nirvana frontman's childhood, career and tragic death, using material from the Cobains' personal archives. Documentary, featuring Kurt Cobain.

programme.title: The Fog of War
programme.desc: Former US Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara looks back over his career, explaining why, after initially supporting the decision to go to war in Vietnam, he eventually chose to speak out against the conflict - a decision that cost him his position. Documentary by acclaimed film-maker Errol Morris.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American film-maker Richard Linklater, whose films include the Before trilogy, School of Rock, Me and Orson Welles, A Scanner Darkly and Boyhood.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: Charting the history of the magazine founded in San Francisco in 1967 by Jann Wenner and Ralph J Gleason that mixed music coverage with innovative political reporting.

programme.title: Discovering: Hugh Grant
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, best-known for his roles in Love Actually, Notting Hill and Four Weddings and a Funeral.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Australian film-maker Phillip Noyce, who directed movies including Dead Calm, Patriot Games, Rabbit-Proof Fence and The Quiet American.

programme.title: Stevie Van Zandt: Disciple
programme.desc: Tracing the musician and actor's career, including his work as a member of Bruce Springsteen's E Street Band and his role as Silvio Dante in The Sopranos.

programme.title: My Icon: Casey Stoney
programme.desc: The football coach talks about her idols.

programme.title: JFK Revisited: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: Oliver Stone's documentary examining the assassination of US president John F Kennedy, using James DiEugenio's 1992 book Destiny Betrayed: JFK, Cuba and the Garrison Case, and recently declassified US government files to shine a light on what happened in Dallas on November 22, 1963. Narrated by Whoopi Goldberg and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: I Am Jackie O
programme.desc: Profile of Jackie Kennedy Onassis, providing a definitive exploration of her life, from first lady to widow, unofficial royalty and fashion icon.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: Documentary about the boy band, featuring rare and exclusive interviews with the members, who talk candidly about how they grappled with the true cost of global fame.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's growing success with their first number one single. But as their popularity soars, so does the tabloid mania.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's split which came to an end when Mikey proposed a reunion. The comeback proved a huge hit - but then tragedy struck and the tabloids descended.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the legendary bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. programme.title: When We Were Kings

programme.title: Queen of Speed

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough

programme.title: Shark with Steve Backshall

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
